title: Uganda Document Preparation (DEMO - Historical)
description: Critical document preparation during Idi Amin's regime (1971-1979). Asian Ugandans were given 90 days to leave in 1972. Many had lived there for generations.
items: Passport from country of origin - even if you were born in Uganda. British passport holders faced additional scrutiny.
Birth certificates for entire family - originals AND certified copies. Government offices were unreliable and sometimes hostile.
Marriage certificate - essential for proving family relationships at borders
Property ownership documents - though these were largely ignored during forced expulsions, keep them for future claims
Bank statements and financial records - limited to what you could carry. Most assets were frozen or confiscated.
Education certificates and professional qualifications - to establish credentials in destination country
Vaccination records - yellow fever certificate mandatory for travel to most destinations
Letters of recommendation from employers - if possible to obtain without arousing suspicion
Keep all documents in waterproof envelope on your person at all times - looting and home invasions were common
Make multiple copies - leave one set with trusted contact outside Uganda if possible
